metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one! I've found some amazing free events in East London, including Women's History Month crafts and a family travel show. Plus, there are fantastic museum activities to explore.

activities.title: Family Craft Saturdays
activities.description: Family craft activities inspired by the Museum's collections. This week, create a badge inspired by the Egyptian women in the ! كل العيون عليها / All Eyes on Her! display, celebrating Women's History Month
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on crafting suitable for little ones, explores different cultures, free and local.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground from Hackney Downs to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Limited parking, public transport recommended

activities.title: Family Traveller Live 2026
activities.description: This travel show brings together destinations, experts and family-friendly inspiration under one roof.
activities.whyItWorks: Discover new destinations for future adventures, interactive displays, and something to spark imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then a local train to Twickenham.
transport.tip: Check the event schedule for specific family activities

activities.title: Sensory Friendly Studio for Families
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.dates: Saturday, 14 March 2026 (Check for similar events this weekend)
activities.description: A monthly workshop for neurodiverse visitors of all ages and their families. Design, create and experiment using a mixture of different art materials in the space. The session and activities are inspired by the museum's collections.
activities.whyItWorks: Relaxed and supportive environment, sensory materials, and creative exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take bus 26, 55, or 48 to Bethnal Green station, then walk a few minutes.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific weekend activities.

activities.title: Young V&A Museum Visit
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore the world of childhood through toys, design, and interactive exhibits. Free drop-in activities (arts and crafts, trails, storytelling and tours) available, check the official website before you visit as times and dates vary throughout the year.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, buggy parking, baby changing facilities, relaxed atmosphere.

transport.fromHackney: Take bus 26, 55, or 48 to Bethnal Green station, then walk a few minutes.
transport.tip: Sundays afternoons are less crowded.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the playgrounds, fountains, and green spaces of the Olympic Park. Enjoy walking trails and open areas for kids to run around.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run, playgrounds, water features (seasonal).

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events and activities.

activities.title: Mini Morris: Birthday Band
activities.dates: 19th March 2026 (Check for similar events this weekend)
activities.description: Create a colourful tambourine.
activities.whyItWorks: Craft activity, tailored to younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Walthamstow Central, then walk a short distance.
transport.tip: Check the gallery website for specific family events this weekend.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A real working farm in the heart of Hackney. Meet farmyard animals, explore the gardens, and learn about where food comes from.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, tactile experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle, depending on your location in Hackney.
transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: Enjoy family fun with amazing shows, dancing, puppetry and free events this Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Various activities, something for everyone, central location.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the tube (Central line) to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the full schedule of events and to book tickets.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration, chocolate reward, historic setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early to avoid crowds

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and the wonders of the natural world. Check out the earthquake simulator and Andy's clock from CBeebies.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Download the museum map to guide your visit.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits on space, technology, and medicine. Check out the dedicated STEM play areas for small children.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, STEM play areas, free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific family activities

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt (included in zoo entry).
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, Easter egg hunt, outdoor activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Euston and walk to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: The event features seven new compositions created by talented students, inspired by Kew's lush spring landscape and world-renowned magnolia and cherry blossoms.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity, beautiful scenery, musical experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond.
transport.tip: Head for Cherry Walk and Magnolia Walk, then finish near The Orangery café.

activities.title: British Library – Fairy Tales
activities.description: This immersive family exhibition invites children to step into the magical world of classic fairy tales through beautiful illustrations, pop-up books, original manuscripts and even puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive elements, explores classic stories, engaging for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Euston.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Battersea Park offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor space, playgrounds, mini zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a local bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check the website for events and activities.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive, encourages exploration, aligns with interests.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggstravaganza: Easter Family Festival
activities.description: Includes Easter trail, crafts and face-painting.
activities.whyItWorks: Craft activity, family festival, variety of activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to London Bridge, then a train to North Dulwich.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Royal Mews Easter Egg Expedition
activities.description: Follow an Easter trail around the working stables at Buckingham Palace. Look out for clues as you explore the historic carriages and courtyard.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploration, Easter trail, historical setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the tube (Central line) to Green Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: A charming puppet show based on the book by Julia Donaldson.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets, engaging storyline, suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Check for age recommendations and book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A theatrical adaptation of the classic story.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging story, family entertainment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then the tube (Central line) to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Check for age recommendations and book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Every day this Easter a Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Themed, food included, tour of London.

transport.fromHackney: Check departure location.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: London Wetland Centre – Gozzle Nature Trail
activities.description: Fun-filled wetland adventure with Gozzle the gosling from Julia Donaldson and Sara Ogilvie's picture book. Follow the interactive trail to discover what it takes Gozzle to become a grown-up goose.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail, outdoor activity, animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then a local bus to Barnes.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration, chocolate reward, historic setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early to avoid crowds.
